page: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is also strong, ranking in the top 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (VNNI and BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. These capabilities are reflected in top-tier results for gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and image and text processing.

The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers strong read and write bandwidth, placing in the top 25% for large-block uncached operations. In database benchmarks, the instance achieves top-tier performance for Passmark database operations and strong throughput for Redis SET operations. For machine learning workloads, the CPU architecture supports fast LLM inference speeds, achieving top-tier token generation rates for small and medium models. Given the compute-heavy 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is suited for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, web serving, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance includes no local storage or GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core capabilities, with top 10% rankings in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, driving top-tier performance in data decompression and database operations. This configuration is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive single-core and database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and database benchmark performance
AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction set support
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and LLM inference
